notes: Historical/temp/legacy signals retained for context only. All records here are low-confidence and non-authoritative.

records.title: Legacy Code And Assets Are Present
records.summary: Repository still includes large legacy folders (`legacy/`, `LEGACY/`) as historical artifacts.
records.description: These are context signals, not active source-of-truth for target USF design.

design_authority.reason: Legacy content is historical and does not define current target model.

records.title: TEMP USF Script Tree Is Large Ontology Draft
records.summary: TEMP USF tree contains 95 files, mostly phenomenon taxonomy and exploratory content scaffolding.
records.description: Large concept breadth but sparse executable integration.
records.owner_direction: Treat as low-authority idea source unless owner confirms specifics.

authoritative_weight.reason: TEMP tree is exploratory and not integrated into canonical runtime contracts.

records.title: Temp Planning/Handoff Docs Provide Historical Design Signals
records.summary: `documents/temp_stuff` currently includes 13 markdown docs spanning handoff, roadmap, and USF plan artifacts.
records.description: These docs are explicitly marked non-canonical context.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in temp docs and temp README.
records.owner_direction: Include as low-confidence record source only.

records.title: Archive Signal: Entrypoint-First Runtime Plan
records.summary: Temp plan proposes strict entrypoint-contract-first runtime shape with deterministic validation and registry compilation.
records.description: Potentially useful design input but not authoritative.

records.title: Archive Signal: Capability Platform Separation Plan
records.summary: Temp plan proposes output channel taxonomy and bridge-registered execution capabilities.
records.description: Aligns conceptually with script-orchestrated Rust capability model.

records.title: Archive Signal: Sampler/Categorizer Generalization Plan
records.summary: Temp plan proposes scale-bound generic sampler/categorizer contracts with explicit kernel ids.

records.title: Archive Signal: Minimal USF Tech Demo Plan
records.summary: Temp plan defines narrow proof objectives for a minimal USF demo.
records.description: Contains deterministic and zone-centric assumptions that may now diverge.

alignment.reason: Some assumptions in this plan conflict with current owner direction.

records.title: Archive Signal: Naming Alignment Review
records.summary: Temp naming review records historical migrations and remaining naming drift concerns.
records.description: Useful for terminology cleanup, not target architecture authority.

records.title: Archive Signal: Draft USF Architecture Contract
records.summary: Draft contract proposes invariants/config model and script-authority framing.
records.description: Document predates latest owner clean-slate direction and may contain conflicting assumptions.

records.title: Archive Signal: Content Scripting Cheat Sheet Draft
records.summary: Cheat-sheet draft captures older content ABI ideas and script archetype proposals.
records.description: Contains mixed legacy terminology; useful as idea source only.

records.title: Archive Signal: Refactor Checklist And Entity-Grounded Note
records.summary: Temp checklist and refactor note describe migration state and invariants from prior refactor phases.
records.description: They are historical progress artifacts, not current authoritative design.

records.title: Archive Signal: Early Channel-Family Realization Layout
records.summary: Current implementation sketches chunk realization via concrete channel families with telemetry.
records.description: This is useful implementation context, but not the target USF contract. It captures an early direction where concrete families (e.g., mesh/material/collider/audio/particles/trigger/simulation_service) route realization outputs.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in USF docs and runtime realization modules.
records.owner_direction: Keep as archival implementation signal only; primary intent is a broader representation/projection/interaction capability substrate contract.

design_authority.reason: Owner direction treats this as partial implementation context, not authoritative USF design.
